questions.question: What is the main purpose of earning badges in Notion Academy?

options.text: To validate and showcase your Notion skills
options.explanation: Badges are awarded when you complete lessons or paths, serving as proof of the abilities you’ve learned.
Badges do not grant access to premium features; they are a recognition tool only.

options.text: To receive discounts on Notion subscriptions
options.explanation: Earning badges does not affect pricing or provide discounts.
Badges are unrelated to storage limits.

questions.question: Which Notion plan type is required to become a Notion Certified Admin?

options.explanation: Certification is not limited to Enterprise; it’s available for any plan.
The article states you can become certified on any plan type at any company.
While a Free plan works, it isn’t the exclusive requirement.

options.text: No Notion plan is needed; certification is external
options.explanation: You still need a Notion account, which is tied to a plan.

questions.question: Which beginner‑level lesson focuses on “Building basics”?

options.explanation: That lesson is about teamwork, not building basics.
The lesson titled “Building basics” is explicitly marked as Beginner.
Custom Agents is a Beginner lesson but its focus is automation, not basic building.
That lesson is Intermediate and about templates.

questions.question: How many individual lessons are included in the Advanced Path?

options.explanation: The Advanced Path contains more than three lessons.
Four lessons belong to the Workflows Path, not the Advanced Path.
The article lists five lessons under the Advanced Path.
There are only five lessons, not six.

questions.question: Which learning path is described as helping users “Create connected systems that unite teams and projects, with the help of AI”?

options.explanation: Essentials Path is for basic skills, not AI‑driven system building.
The Workflows Path description includes that exact phrase about AI‑assisted connected systems.
Advanced Path focuses on automation and architecture, not the specific AI‑connected description.
Custom Agents is a single lesson, not a multi‑lesson path.

questions.question: Among the lessons listed, which has the longest estimated duration?

options.explanation: At 40 minutes, this lesson is longer than any other listed duration.
30 minutes is shorter than the 40‑minute Templates & marketplace lesson.
20 minutes is less than both 30 and 40 minutes.
15 minutes is the shortest among the options.

questions.question: The “Project management” lesson belongs to which category?

options.explanation: Essentials is a broader path, but the lesson is tagged under Workflows.
The article lists “Project management” as an Intermediate lesson under the Workflows category.
Advanced lessons are separate; this one is not labeled as such.
Badged Path is a type of learning track, not the specific category for this lesson.

questions.question: If a learner wants to focus on “automating work” and “workspace architecture best practices,” which path should they choose and what is its difficulty level?

options.explanation: Workflows Path covers connected systems, not specifically automation and architecture best practices.
The Advanced Path description mentions automating work and learning workspace architecture best practices, and it is labeled Advanced.
Essentials Path is for basic skills, not the advanced topics described.
Custom Agents is a single lesson, not a full path covering both topics.

questions.question: Which lesson combines “forms, charts, and automations” to build an intake system, and what are its difficulty level and duration?

options.text: An issue tracker for any team – Intermediate – 15 min
options.explanation: The description explicitly says the lesson teaches building an intake system using forms, charts, and automations; it is marked Intermediate and lasts 15 minutes.
Project management focuses on setting up tasks, not on forms, charts, and automations.
Custom Agents is about automating repeat work, but does not mention forms and charts.
Templates & marketplace deals with creating and selling templates, not intake systems.

questions.question: Which learning offering explicitly mentions the use of AI in its description?

options.explanation: Custom Agents is about automation but the description does not mention AI.
The Workflows Path description includes “with the help of AI.”
Project management focuses on task databases and does not reference AI.
Essentials Path is for basic skills and contains no AI reference.
